research_context.market: Hong Kong product teams evaluating AI user or synthetic user research tools
research_context.purpose: Generate a reusable V5 persona who is genuinely relevant to founder-side product discovery and concept validation without pre-encoding any conclusion about synthetic users.
research_context.platform_rule: Build a person first. Product-building context may include stable role facts and working habits, but must not pre-encode whether the person supports or rejects AI users.
forbidden_core_shortcuts: already believes synthetic users can replace interviews
needs AI validation because no users exist

fixed.occupation: Solo founder building a workflow SaaS product after several years in product design and startup work

preferred.persona_generation_rule: Generate the person from ordinary life, values, routines, constraints, and working habits first. Founder context should be factual and reusable, not concept-shaped.
preferred.sample_eligibility_rule: The person should plausibly make early product decisions, frame hypotheses, review research evidence, and decide whether to spend scarce time or money on validation.
preferred.product_context_rule: Use product-building context only for stable role facts such as work history, evidence habits, collaboration style, and shipping constraints. Do not write synthetic-user conclusions into persona core.
preferred.career_path_expectation: Should include real product design experience before founder work, with enough exposure to weak assumptions, customer ambiguity, and prototype iteration to make product-critique behavior plausible.
variation_targets: private builder versus public founder persona
interview respect without formal research training
design craft orientation versus shipping pressure

human_difference_axes_expectations.control_preference: Should lean high but not absolute; the person should want interpretive control over evidence and product direction.
human_difference_axes_expectations.trust_style: Should not default to trusting AI or distrusting people; trust should depend on transparency, challenge quality, and fit to real founder work.
human_difference_axes_expectations.complexity_tolerance: Can be medium to high when it helps real decisions.
human_difference_axes_expectations.decision_tempo: Should show the tension between moving fast and fearing wasted build time.
human_difference_axes_expectations.financial_attention_cadence: Should reflect small-business or solo-founder budget vigilance.
human_difference_axes_expectations.relationship_to_money: Money may represent runway, optionality, and proof of seriousness.
human_difference_axes_expectations.need_for_explanation: Should want reasons, not only answers.
human_difference_axes_expectations.guidance_preference: May want structured pushback rather than step-by-step hand-holding.

negative_prompting: Do not make the founder a caricature of startup hype.
Do not force venture-backed language or Silicon Valley mannerisms.
Do not assume they hate interviews or have no access to real users.
Do not make every frustration about AI.
